AuthDescriptor.description: How a connector authenticates against its upstream vendor API.
AuthDescriptor.title: AuthDescriptor

ConnectorAuthModel.description: Supported authentication families a connector can declare.
ConnectorAuthModel.title: ConnectorAuthModel

ConnectorCompat.description: Spec compatibility range the connector claims to support.
ConnectorCompat.title: ConnectorCompat

DryRunScope.description: What a connector's dry-run mode validates.
DryRunScope.title: DryRunScope

EgressDescriptor.description: What response actions this connector can execute on the upstream vendor.
EgressDescriptor.title: EgressDescriptor

EnrichmentDescriptor.description:
What canonical artifacts this connector can PRODUCE on what subjects.

Read-side analogue of :class:`EgressDescriptor`. Where egress
declares verbs (which response actions the connector implements),
enrichment declares **shapes** : which canonical artifact types
the connector produces (the ``artifact_type`` strings that ride
in :class:`~warlog_spec.artifacts.ArtifactEnvelope`), on which
entity / IOC kinds it can be invoked, with what freshness, and
whether it supports bulk lookups.

A connector with empty enrichment (default) declares "I don't do
read-side enrichment" — the runtime won't route enrichment
requests to it. A connector with non-empty enrichment is
expected to implement :class:`~warlog_spec.abi.AbiEnricher`
and return :class:`~warlog_spec.artifacts.EnrichmentAssessment`
(or another envelope-bearing canonical shape).

The ``produces_artifact_types`` vocabulary is intentionally open
(free-form strings) at this stage of the spec ; common values
today are ``"enrichment.context"``, ``"ioc.reputation"``,
``"mitre.assessment"``. A registry / enum can land later if a
canonical set crystallizes.

supportsEntityTypes.description: EntityType values the enricher can resolve. Open list to keep the spec resilient against EntityType growth ; concrete enricher implementations MUST match values to the canonical EntityType enum at runtime.
supportsEntityTypes.title: Supportsentitytypes

supportsIocTypes.description: IOCType values the enricher can resolve.
supportsIocTypes.title: Supportsioctypes

bulkLookup.description: True when the enricher can accept N subjects in a single call and amortize the per-call cost. The runtime SHOULD batch subjects into bulk calls when this is True.
bulkLookup.title: Bulklookup

EnrichmentDescriptor.title: EnrichmentDescriptor

FreshnessHint.description:
Hint about how fresh the data an enricher returns is.

The runtime / orchestrator uses this to decide caching policy and
whether to re-query when an enriched artifact ages out. It is
NOT a guarantee — vendors with stronger or weaker freshness can
document the actual SLA in their connector's prose.
FreshnessHint.title: FreshnessHint

IngressDelivery.description: How a connector delivers events to the runtime.
IngressDelivery.title: IngressDelivery

IngressDescriptor.description: What the connector pulls/receives FROM the upstream vendor.
IngressDescriptor.title: IngressDescriptor

LifecycleDescriptor.description: Connector lifecycle features the runtime can rely on.
LifecycleDescriptor.title: LifecycleDescriptor

ResponseActionId.description:
Catalog of canonical, vendor-neutral atomic response actions.

Connectors declare which of these they implement via
``ConnectorCapability.egress.supports_response_actions``. New
actions require an RFC and a MINOR spec bump.
ResponseActionId.title: ResponseActionId

description:
Declarative manifest a connector publishes about itself.

A connector can be write-only (``egress`` populated, ``enrichment``
empty), read-only (``enrichment`` populated, ``egress`` empty), or
bi-directional (both populated). The runtime routes requests
based on the populated descriptors.
title: ConnectorCapability
